name: general-workshop
summary: A room where things are made or repaired — benches, tools arranged by trade, work-in-progress half-finished on every surface.

size_guidance.aspect_ratio: rectangular, benches along walls, clear working center

primary_palette.note: smith's workshop — forge, anvil, bellows arranged for iron work
carpenter's or joiner's shop — benches and tool walls
alchemist's lab-workshop — glass, fire, and labeled jars
textile workshop — weaving and thread work
mixed-trade repair shop — sharpening, fixing, fitting

story_prompts: active smithy with work still on the anvil, fire banked not dead
an alchemist's lab mid-experiment, something bubbling
abandoned workshop, tools rusting, a half-finished chair on the bench
cultist-run workshop making ritual implements on standard carpentry equipment
a dwarf's trade shop far from home, precise and immaculate
anti_patterns: don't mix unrelated trades without a reason — smithing and weaving together looks random
don't forget work-in-progress — an empty bench looks wrong
avoid rugs or fine carpets; floors are dirty and scuffed
